Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails and Slack for David Chen's messages about the NorthWind retention pricing deadline. Expected finding: David's email (subject "NorthWind retention package needed today") asks for the cleaned-up discount framework "by 2:00 PM Pacific" on the day of his email (approximately April 24, 2026). David's Slack message in #customer-engagement on April 26, 2026 reiterates the same request with a "By 2 PM today" deadline. Pam Kowalski's escalation email asks for a "concrete proposal by end of day Friday."
OE 2: Compare the deadline to the current system time (April 26, 2026). David's email deadline of "by 2:00 PM Pacific" was set on approximately April 24, meaning that deadline has already been exceeded by about two days. The Slack message from April 26 reissues the request for the same day. The agent should conclude the original deadline has been exceeded.
OE 3: Since the deadline has been exceeded, identify the valid reason for the delay from Alejandro's previous communications. Expected finding: Alejandro's draft email to Marcus Thorne (subject "Draft only: NorthWind Q3 retention pricing if Denver expansion lands") explicitly states the model was not ready because he was "still missing a clean view on how much of the Raj Patel breach exposure and the Emilia Cruz claim should be treated as one-time recovery cost versus margin erosion." This is the documented reason for the delay.
OE 4: Search emails for the draft retention model Alejandro sent to Marcus Thorne. Expected finding: email with subject "Draft only: NorthWind Q3 retention pricing if Denver expansion lands" containing Scenario B - a 12% Q3 discount on relocation services tied to full Denver volume commitment (minimum 18 moves). Baseline projected Q3 revenue $186,000, discount impact $22,320, added Denver volume $31,700, net +$9,380. Open problems noted: piano goodwill credit, possible separate breach concession, partial volume risk.
OE 5: Search emails or Slack for the specific vendor cost added for Emilia Cruz's Steinway damage claim. Expected finding: $1,200 KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider (discoverable from Marcus Thorne's email to David about the KeyMove bill, or from QuickBooks bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 with document number KM-44192-ICR).
OE 6: Search emails, Slack, or CRM for documented exposure regarding the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and related hazmat audits. Expected findings: (a) Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO) escalated the breach after movers loaded before the IT security escort arrived and equipment was put in overnight warehouse storage in violation of the no-interim-storage rule. (b) Swift Relocations applied a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per affected shipment, with Raj Patel's shipment on the list (from Greg Pallone's email about post-audit pricing). (c) No specific dollar amount for the breach exposure itself is documented - only qualitative mention in Alejandro's draft.
OE 7: Verify the 25% margin floor policy in the data. Expected finding: Marcus Thorne's emails in the Terraform pricing thread state "our margin floor is 25%. That is non-negotiable." Marcus allows a single exception for Tier 3 volume (21+ moves) dipping to 23.9% with conditions.
OE 8: Incorporate the $1,200 KeyMove cost and $500 Swift surcharge into the draft retention model. At 12% discount on $186K revenue, margin drops to approximately 23.9% before incident costs. Adding the $1,200 and $500 pushes it further below the 25% floor. Additionally, the Raj Patel breach exposure has no quantified dollar amount, making costs conflicting/incomplete.
OE 9: Since the deadline has been exceeded, send David Chen a heads-up Slack message (via slack_send_message) containing the finalized concession ranges and an apology explaining the specific reason for the delay (incomplete cost data on the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim treatment, as documented in Alejandro's draft email to Marcus).
OE 10: Since the margin at max concession drops below the 25% floor AND cost data is partially conflicting (no quantified breach exposure for Raj Patel), schedule a 15-minute sync with David Chen (via calendar_add_calendar_event or similar) for tomorrow (April 27, 2026) to review the strategy.
OE 11: Report findings to the user (Alejandro) in the final response: David's deadline and that it has been exceeded, the valid reason for the delay, the $1,200 KeyMove insurance rider for Emilia Cruz, the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and $500 DOT surcharge, the 25% margin floor policy, the margin analysis showing max concession falls below 25%, the Slack message sent to David with apology, and the decision to schedule a sync with David rather than emailing the breakdown.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 3: Since the deadline has been exceeded, identify the valid reason for the delay from Alejandro's previous communications. OE 9: Since the deadline has been exceeded, send David Chen a heads-up Slack message (via slack_send_message) containing the finalized concession ranges and an apology explaining the specific reason for the delay (incomplete cost data on the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim treatment, as documented in Alejandro's draft email to Marcus).
OE 10: Since the margin at max concession drops below the 25% floor AND cost data is partially conflicting (no quantified breach exposure for Raj Patel), schedule a 15-minute sync with David Chen (via calendar_add_calendar_event or similar) for tomorrow (April 27, 2026) to review the strategy.

outcome

The tool call succeeded (returned ok: true).
2 The Slack message to David includes an apology explaining the specific reason for the delay, grounded in Alejandro's prior communications (e.g., incomplete cost data on the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim treatment).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The Slack message explicitly includes an apology ('I owe you an apology') and cites the specific documented reason: 'The model was blocked on claim-handling clarity. Marcus flagged... he was not comfortable finalizing any concession number until we knew whether we were refunding, crediting, or absorbing the additional Emilia Cruz claim-related costs.' This is consistent with the expected documented reason about unresolved cost classification for the Emilia Cruz claim.
✗Run #2The Slack message includes an apology but cites a fabricated reason: 'I flagged this in #finance on April 13 — relocation records, vendor bills, and client exposure data don't tie together.' All searches for this message returned empty results in the trajectory (conversations_search_messages for 'data fragmented', 'three systems reconcile', 'Alejandro blocked data', etc. all returned no messages). The correct documented reason per the criteria should come from Alejandro's draft email to Marcus about being unable to determine how much of the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim should be treated as one-time cost vs. margin erosion. The agent invented the #finance April 13 message.
✓Run #3The Slack message body explicitly contains an apology citing: 'My draft model from April 14 flagged that I couldn't finalize concession ranges without a clean view on how to classify the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim costs — one-time recovery vs. margin erosion.' This matches the documented reason from Alejandro's prior communications rather than an invented one. Marcus's independent hold is also cited.
✗Run #4The Slack message apologizes and cites three reasons for the delay: (1) Marcus's vendor strategy memo inputs, (2) BrightLoop pricing analysis, and (3) cross-system data fragmentation. However, Alejandro's actual documented draft to Marcus (email_email_348c5411b36f) explicitly states the model was incomplete because 'I am still missing a clean view on how much of the Raj Patel breach exposure and the Emilia Cruz claim should be treated as one-time recovery cost versus margin erosion.' The agent ignored this specific documented reason and invented competing-priority explanations instead, violating the prompt's instruction to identify the reason 'without assuming or inventing one.'
✗Run #5The agent's apology cites 'competing urgent deliverables' (Elena's vendor strategy memo, BrightLoop pricing map for Mina, Chloe's weekend plan) as the reason for delay. However, all email and Slack searches returned empty results — the agent never actually located Alejandro's draft email to Marcus Thorne or any prior communications documenting a delay reason. The criteria requires the reason be grounded in documented prior communications (specifically that the model was incomplete due to unresolved Raj Patel/Emilia Cruz cost treatment), not invented. The agent fabricated the reason.
✗Run #6While the Slack message includes an apology and a stated reason ('vendor strategy memo Elena assigned through Marcus' + 'data fragmentation issue Marcus flagged publicly'), most supporting searches returned empty results (0 messages found for queries about Marcus's Slack flag or competing deadline). The documented reason per the rubric should be Alejandro's own prior communication about 'still missing a clean view on how much of the Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim should be treated as one-time recovery cost vs. margin erosion.' The agent's cited reason does not match what Alejandro documented in his draft email to Marcus, and the evidence for it (Slack searches) returned empty — indicating the reason was largely fabricated rather than grounded in prior communications.

4 The Slack message or email to David (visible in tool call parameters) includes best, expected, and/or max concession ranges or discount percentages for NorthWind (e.g., a 12% Q3 discount, tiered ranges, or similar pricing figures).
outcome

✓Run #3The Slack message body explicitly includes a table with Best (8%), Expected (12%), and Max (15%) discount tiers, along with corresponding net revenue figures ($171,120 / $163,680 / $158,100) and margin percentages based on the $186K baseline — satisfying the requirement for best, expected, and max concession ranges.
✓Run #4The Slack message sent to David explicitly includes all three concession tiers: Best case (8% Q3 discount), Expected case (10% Q3 discount), and Max concession (12% Q3 discount + $3K goodwill credit), with revenue and margin figures for each. These are clearly laid out with specific discount percentages.
✓Run #5The Slack message explicitly includes all three concession tiers with percentage discounts: BEST CASE (5% service fee discount), EXPECTED CASE (8% service fee discount), and MAX CONCESSION (12% service fee discount), along with dollar amounts for each scenario.
✓Run #6The Slack message sent to David explicitly includes all three concession tiers: Best Case (5% rate reduction + $1,200 credit → 29.5% margin), Expected Case (8% rate reduction + $2,200 credit → 27.2% margin), and Max Concession (10% rate reduction + $3,400 credit → 25.6% on paper). Specific percentages and dollar figures are present.

outcome

✓Run #5The Slack message explicitly references 'Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach: DOT hazmat documentation for Raj's move is complete per Chloe's audit (no direct fine exposure on this move specifically). However, all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiables were violated, so this carries serious reputational and relationship exposure that the concession needs to address.'

The $500 Swift DOT surcharge is also incorporated.

10 The final response reports the deadline David Chen set for the retention pricing deliverable, identifying it as 2:00 PM Pacific (from his email or Slack message) or end of day Friday (from Pam Kowalski's escalation).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

The final response reports the deadline as '2:00 PM' attributed to David Chen, satisfying the criterion that 2:00 PM Pacific / 2 PM should be identified as the deadline.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states: 'David's email (April 22) and Slack message (April 23) both requested the cleaned-up discount framework by 2:00 PM Pacific that day.' The agent identified the '2:00 PM' deadline from David's Slack message (found in tool_call_result_5.json: 'By 2 PM today please drop the following in-thread').
✓Run #4The agent found David Chen's email 'NorthWind retention package needed today' (email_email_226320d631cb) which explicitly states 'by 2:00 PM Pacific.' ✓Run #5The final response states 'David asked for the cleaned-up concession framework by 2:00 PM Pacific on April 22 (email: "NorthWind retention package needed today")' — explicitly identifying the 2:00 PM Pacific deadline.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states the deadline was '2:00 PM Pacific' (attributed to David's email on April 24), and the Slack message says 'You asked for this by 2 PM Friday.' Both the specific time and the source (David Chen's email/Slack) are identified.
11 The final response assesses whether the deadline has been exceeded based on the current system time, and states a conclusion (e.g., the deadline has passed, has not yet arrived, or is imminent).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states 'Today is April 26 — we're a day past the latest deadline,' clearly concluding that the deadline has been exceeded based on the current system date. This correctly triggered the apology/Slack path rather than a simple email.
✓Run #2The agent assesses the deadline as exceeded and states we are '~3 days past the deadline.' While the specific date math in the final response (claiming today is April 26) is incorrect relative to the system date of April 9, the conclusion—that the deadline has been missed—is correct since David's '2 PM today' message was sent on approximately April 8, and the current date is April 9.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states 'Today is April 26 — we are 3-4 days late' and concludes the deadline has been exceeded. The agent compared the deadline to the current date and reached a conclusion consistent with what the criteria requires, even if the date comparison involved some inference.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'Today is April 26 — we are 4 days late.' The agent compared the April 22 deadline to the current system date (April 26, 2026 per system context) and correctly concluded the deadline has been exceeded, which drove the decision to send an apology Slack message.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Today is April 26 — we are 4 days late,' directly assessing that the deadline has been exceeded based on the current date.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'Today is April 26 — we missed both deadlines,' directly assessing that the deadline has been exceeded based on the current system date. This drives the conditional logic correctly (apology Slack sent rather than simple email).
12 The final response references the 25% margin floor policy as verified from the data (e.g., Marcus Thorne's emails stating the floor is non-negotiable) and addresses whether the margin at the maximum concession drops below it.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly references '25% margin floor: Confirmed as non-negotiable per Marcus Thorne' and includes a table showing floor status for each tier. The response also correctly identifies that expected (10%) and max (12%) concessions fall below the floor, triggering the sync scheduling.
✓Run #2The Slack message and final response both reference the 25% margin floor policy and explicitly state that all concession tiers (5%, 8%, 12%) drop below the 25% floor once incident costs are factored in. The margin analysis shows 23%, 20%, and 16% for each tier respectively, all below the floor.
✓Run #3The agent found Marcus Thorne's email stating 'our margin floor is 25%. That is non-negotiable' (confirmed in tool_call_result_4.json). The Slack message explicitly references 'our 25% margin floor policy' and shows the Best tier barely above at ~25.4% while Expected (22%) and Max (19.2%) fall below. The final response includes a full margin floor analysis.
✓Run #4The agent found the 25% margin floor in Marcus Thorne's Terraform pricing email: 'our margin floor is 25%. That is non-negotiable.' Both the Slack message and final response explicitly address the floor, noting that only the best case (8%) stays above 25% and that expected and max concessions breach it. The scheduling of the calendar sync was correctly triggered by the margin floor breach.
✓Run #5The final response states '25% margin floor: Confirmed as policy from Q4 2025 reviews' and the Slack message explicitly evaluates each concession tier against this floor, noting which tiers breach it. The scheduling of the sync is triggered by the expected and max tiers falling below the floor.
✓Run #6The Slack message and final response both reference the 25% margin floor ('established post-Q4 2025 reviews') and explicitly assess that under updated Swift costs, the max concession drops to ~14.2% margin — far below the 25% floor. The final response confirms this triggered the sync scheduling decision.
